page: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with x86_64 architecture. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The hardware configuration includes a bundled 1229 GB local SSD and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench image processing scoring in the top 25%. Memory performance is also notable, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25%, and uncached large-block memory read bandwidth reaching 76.87 GB/sec. However, multi-core processing and database operations fall into the average tier. While small-model LLM text generation achieves top-tier speeds at 334.98 tokens/sec, large-model prompt processing is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32ads_v5 offers high memory density and integrated local storage, reducing the need for external storage attachments for memory-intensive workloads. The primary tradeoff is its average multi-core scaling and lack of hardware acceleration, making it less suitable for heavy GPU-bound tasks or massive parallel compute jobs. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring high RAM capacity and local scratch space.
description: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it includes a bundled 1229 GB local SSD and supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and memory subsystem scores, placing in the top 25%, while multi-core operations and database processing remain average. Large-model LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% for 70B models. Qualitatively, the instance provides high memory density and integrated storage capacity, making it cost-effective for memory-bound workloads without requiring external storage add-ons. It is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ads_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 1229 GB local SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory performance for databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density, dedicated AMD EPYC processing, and integrated local SSD storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM and a 1229 GB local SSD
Strong single-core CPU and memory performance in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average multi-core scaling and weak large-model LLM inference
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, caching, and enterprise apps
